name: Androgen sensor (AR + CRISPRa)
shortDescription: Detects androgens (testosterone and anabolic-steroid mimics) in water and sport/clinical samples using the human androgen receptor expressed in GRAS yeast — a eukaryotic sensing class bacteria cannot do — gating a CRISPRa circuit driving a fluorescent reporter.

input.detects: Androgenic activity in water, clinical, or anti-doping samples
input.operatingRange: Yeast androgen screen detects sub-nM to nM DHT/testosterone

sensing.sensorMechanism: Ligand-bound human androgen receptor (AR) binds androgen-response elements (AREs), activating an ARE promoter (the yeast androgen screen).
sensing.signalFlow: Androgen -> AR binds ARE promoter -> sgRNA -> dCas9-VPR amplifies the reporter (CRISPRa) -> fluorescence rises with androgenic activity.

parts.name: Human androgen receptor (AR)
parts.source: Human AR; Sohoni & Sumpter 1998 (yeast androgen screen)

parts.name: sgRNA
parts.source: designed against / upstream of the reporter promoter

performance.notes: The yeast androgen screen (AR/ARE) is a validated assay; the CRISPRa-amplified version is a design template. Reports total androgenic activity, useful for unknown mixtures.

safety.notes: GRAS baker's yeast; eukaryotic chassis enables human-receptor sensing.

buildSteps.title: Design sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target/position the sgRNA; check S. cerevisiae off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: Sensor -> sgRNA; dCas9; reporter. Use the host's standard toolkit.

buildSteps.title: Transform S. cerevisiae
buildSteps.description: Transform, select, and confirm low background.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Incubate with the sample plus a androgen standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the yeast androgen screen (AR/ARE) with eukaryotic CRISPRa
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
